citation.description: Directory contains python/ (pyproject.toml + src/main.py) and typescript/ (pyproject.toml + src/main.ts). Runtime initialization in metrics_polyglot_support.py creates the git repo and rebar ticket store from these fixtures.

criteria.criterion: Fixtures: one Python, one non-Python (Go or TS), each with a minimal rebar ticket-store shape.

citation.description: test_metrics_cli_reports_polyglot_code_health_values is parametrized over python and typescript. It uses fake_analyzer_bin (process-level scc/jscpd stand-ins) to prove the full adapter/registry/CLI pipeline computes code_health values for both languages. The oracle comments explicitly endorse this always-run fake-tool approach as equivalent to skip-if-absent with real tools.

criteria.criterion: Analyzers present (integration, skip-if-absent): code_health computes for BOTH languages.

citation.description: test_metrics_cli_names_missing_analyzers_without_fabricating_zeroes is parametrized over python and typescript. Uses git_only_bin (no scc/jscpd) and asserts module_size_distribution and duplication_summary carry unavailable.reason (not value), with no Errno and no fabricated zeroes. churn and agent_process metrics still compute.

criteria.criterion: Extra/tools absent (always-run): code_health reports Unavailable for both — no crash, no fabricated data.

citation.description: Both test_metrics_polyglot.py (lines 51-62) and test_metrics_polyglot_heldout.py (lines 46-57) assert churn (insertions/deletions), refactor_to_addition_ratio (git-delta), attempts_per_ticket, and first_pass_rate (agent_process) for both Python and TypeScript fixtures.

criteria.criterion: agent_process + git-delta metrics compute for both.

citation.description: test_metrics_polyglot.py covers the analyzers-present path for both languages; test_metrics_polyglot_heldout.py covers the analyzers-absent path for both languages. Both are marked pytest.mark.interface and use real CLI subprocess over disposable copies of the fixture projects.

criteria.criterion: Interface/E2E test covering both fixtures and both present/absent states.
